vulnerabilities.description: Sentry DSN is publicly exposed in code. While this is acceptable (DSNs are meant to be public), it reveals organization and project IDs. Monitor for suspicious error reports.
CSP allows 'unsafe-inline' for scripts. Required for Vercel Analytics integration but increases XSS attack surface. Consider using nonce-based CSP instead.
CSP allows 'unsafe-inline' for styles. Used by Tailwind CSS. Could be mitigated with subresource integrity checks.
No input validation on set() method values. If these values are later used in template contexts or evaluated, could create injection vector. Local use mitigates risk.
No input validation on context values stored in session.context. If used in unsafe contexts later, could cause issues. Currently low risk as data is local.

strengths: Proper use of environment variables for all secrets (GROQ_API_KEY, FINNHUB_API_KEY, Upstash credentials)
No hardcoded secrets or API keys found
Safe URL parameter handling using searchParams.set() (prevents injection)
No eval() or Function() usage - custom safe math parser instead
Proper use of execFileSync over execSync to prevent shell injection
Comprehensive input validation on all API endpoints
Rate limiting implemented across search endpoints
ALTCHA challenge system with proper secret handling (fails closed in production)
Good error handling - doesn't leak sensitive information
Proper CORS restrictions and security headers
No SQL injection risks (no database queries in provided code)
Sentry configured to strip PII (IP addresses, emails)
recommendations: Consider using nonce-based CSP for inline scripts instead of 'unsafe-inline' if possible
Add input validation wrapper for memory.js and session.js helper functions
Monitor Sentry for suspicious error patterns that might indicate attacks
Run 'npm audit' regularly to check for vulnerable dependencies
Keep all API keys (GROQ, Finnhub, Brave Search) properly rotated and never committed
Consider adding request signing/HMAC validation for sensitive API endpoints
Audit access logs for rate limit violations (already detecting in code)
Use HTTPS only (already configured via HSTS header)
Review and audit honeypot/bot detection logic periodically
Consider implementing additional bot detection beyond the 'website' honeypot field
